tools.name: validate_jac
tools.description: Validate Jac code with full type checking. IMPORTANT: You MUST call this tool after generating or modifying any Jac code to verify it compiles correctly. Returns structured errors and warnings. If errors are returned, fix the code and re-validate before presenting it to the user.

tools.name: check_syntax
tools.description: Quick syntax-only check (parse-only, no type checking). Use this for a fast first pass when iterating on code. For final validation, use validate_jac instead.

tools.name: format_jac
tools.description: Format Jac code according to standard style. Use this to ensure code follows Jac formatting conventions before presenting it to the user.

tools.name: py_to_jac
tools.description: Convert Python code to Jac using the official compiler transpiler. Use this as a starting point when migrating Python code — the output may need manual refinement for idiomatic Jac style.

tools.name: explain_error
tools.description: Explain a Jac compiler error with suggestions and examples. Use this when validate_jac returns errors you need to understand. Returns a structured explanation with the error category, root cause, and corrected code pattern.

tools.name: list_examples
tools.description: List available Jac example code categories. Use this to discover working Jac code examples that demonstrate correct patterns before writing new code.

tools.name: get_example
tools.description: Get all .jac files from an example category. Use this to study working code before writing similar functionality — these examples are tested and verified.

tools.name: search_docs
tools.description: Search Jac documentation by keyword. Use this when you are unsure about Jac syntax or features — the results come from the official documentation and are authoritative. Returns ranked snippets with resource URIs.

tools.name: get_ast
tools.description: Parse Jac code and return AST representation. Useful for understanding code structure.

tools.name: lint_jac
tools.description: Lint Jac code to detect style violations, unused symbols, undefined names, unreachable code, and other issues. Supports 30+ lint rules (W3001-W3036). Use auto_fix=true to automatically fix violations and return corrected code.

auto_fix.description: Auto-fix lint violations and return corrected code

tools.name: jac_to_py
tools.description: Convert Jac code to Python using the official compiler transpiler. Useful for understanding what Python code Jac compiles to, or for debugging. The output shows the generated Python equivalent of the Jac source.

code.description: Jac source code to convert to Python

tools.name: jac_to_js
tools.description: Convert Jac code to JavaScript/ECMAScript using the official compiler transpiler. Critical for jac-client full-stack development to understand what JS is generated from .cl.jac client code.

code.description: Jac source code to convert to JavaScript

tools.name: run_jac
tools.description: Execute Jac code and return stdout/stderr output. Use this to verify runtime behavior after validation. Supports running the whole module or a specific entrypoint (walker or function). Code runs in a sandboxed environment with a timeout.

entrypoint.description: Optional specific walker or function to invoke (like jac enter)

tools.name: graph_visualize
tools.description: Execute Jac code and generate a graph visualization of the resulting object-spatial graph. Returns DOT or JSON format. Essential for debugging walker traversal, node connectivity, and data flow in Jac's graph-based programming model.

code.description: Jac source code that builds a graph

tools.name: list_commands
tools.description: List all available jac CLI commands including plugin-provided ones. Returns name, help, group, source, and extended_by for each command. Call get_command(name) to get full argument details before executing.

tools.name: get_command
tools.description: Get full details for a specific jac CLI command including all arguments (name, type, kind, required, default, help). Use this after list_commands to get the argument spec before calling execute_command.

tools.name: execute_command
tools.description: Execute a jac CLI command by name with optional arguments. Use list_commands to discover commands, get_command(name) to get its argument spec, then call this. Arguments are passed as a list of strings exactly as you would type them on the command line (e.g. ['hello.jac', '--no-cache']).

args.description: Arguments to pass to the command, as a list of strings (e.g. ['hello.jac', '--no-cache'])
